query: Vulnerability to climate-related vector-borne infectious diseases in Europe

t1.1.name: Mosquito-Borne Disease Vectors in Europe
t1.1.description: Focuses on Aedes, Anopheles, and Culex species spreading diseases like dengue, chikungunya, West Nile virus, and malaria, including their climate-driven emergence patterns.

t1.2.name: Tick-Borne Disease Vectors and Climate Sensitivity
t1.2.description: Analyzes Ixodes species and other ticks transmitting Lyme disease, tick-borne encephalitis, and Crimean-Congo hemorrhagic fever in relation to climate-driven habitat changes.

t1.name: Climate Change Impacts on Vector Biology and Distribution
t1.description: Examines how climate variables (temperature, precipitation, humidity) affect vector species' lifecycle, survival, reproduction rates, and geographic range expansion across Europe.

t2.name: Vulnerable Populations and Geographic Regions
t2.description: Identifies demographic groups, socioeconomic factors, and geographic areas in Europe that face heightened vulnerability to vector-borne disease transmission under climate change scenarios.

t3.name: Surveillance and Early Warning Systems
t3.description: Reviews existing and proposed monitoring frameworks, predictive models, and integrated surveillance systems for detecting and forecasting climate-related vector-borne disease risks in Europe.

t1.1.name: Mosquito-Borne Disease Vectors
t1.1.description: Focuses on mosquito species (Aedes, Anopheles, Culex) spreading northward in Europe due to warming temperatures and altered precipitation patterns.

t1.2.name: Tick-Borne Disease Vectors
t1.2.description: Addresses expansion of tick populations (Ixodes species) and tick-borne pathogens including Lyme disease and tick-borne encephalitis across European regions.

t1.name: Climate Change and Vector Distribution
t1.description: Examines how climate change parameters (temperature, precipitation, humidity) affect the geographic distribution, abundance, and seasonal activity of disease vectors in Europe.

t2.1.name: Urban and Peri-urban Vulnerability
t2.1.description: Examines how urbanization patterns, heat islands, water storage practices, and population density create specific vulnerabilities in European cities and surrounding areas.

t2.name: Population Vulnerability and Risk Factors
t2.description: Analyzes demographic, socioeconomic, and health system factors that determine differential vulnerability of European populations to vector-borne disease risks.

t3.1.name: Climate-Informed Risk Prediction Models
t3.1.description: Describes forecasting tools that integrate climate projections with epidemiological and entomological data to predict future vector-borne disease risk in European regions.

t3.name: Surveillance and Early Warning Systems
t3.description: Covers monitoring systems, predictive models, and integrated surveillance approaches used across Europe to detect and respond to vector-borne disease threats.

t2.2.description: Analyzes how urban environments, particularly those with aging populations, inadequate infrastructure, or heat islands, create conditions for increased vector-borne disease risk.

t2.description: Identifies European populations, regions, and communities at heightened risk due to demographic, socioeconomic, health system, or environmental factors that increase vulnerability to vector-borne diseases.
